title: Mastodon environment configuration
description: Environment variables recognised by a Mastodon instance.

LOCAL_DOMAIN.description: The domain name of the server. This is the identifier used in ActivityPub and cannot be changed after initial setup without breaking federation.

WEB_DOMAIN.description: Override the domain used to construct public URLs. Useful when the ActivityPub domain differs from the HTTP hostname (e.g. you serve Mastodon from a sub-path). Defaults to LOCAL_DOMAIN.

ALTERNATE_DOMAINS.description: Comma-separated list of additional domain names that the server accepts HTTP Host headers for.

STREAMING_API_BASE_URL.description: Base URL for the streaming API (WebSocket). Computed automatically from WEB_DOMAIN / LOCAL_DOMAIN when absent.

LIMITED_FEDERATION_MODE.description: Restrict federation to explicitly allowed servers only.

SINGLE_USER_MODE.description: Disable registrations and redirect the landing page to the admin account profile.

SELF_DESTRUCT.description: When set, puts the instance into self-destruct mode: all local content is removed and federation partners are notified. The value must match a token generated by the CLI. Cannot be undone.

EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES.description: Space-separated list of opt-in experimental feature flags to enable.

UPDATE_CHECK_URL.description: URL polled to check for new Mastodon releases. Set to an empty string to disable update checks.

DONATION_CAMPAIGNS_URL.description: URL of the donation campaigns API. When set, Mastodon may display fundraising notices to admins.

DONATION_CAMPAIGNS_ENVIRONMENT.description: Environment tag sent with donation-campaign API requests.

SOURCE_TAG.description: Git tag of the running source code. Shown in the about page and version API.

SOURCE_BASE_URL.description: Base URL of the source code repository. Used to construct links on the about page.

GITHUB_REPOSITORY.description: GitHub repository identifier used to construct source code links.

SOURCE_COMMIT.description: Git commit SHA of the running source code. Shown in the version API.

MASTODON_VERSION_PRERELEASE.description: Prerelease suffix appended to the Mastodon version string (e.g. "beta.1").

MASTODON_VERSION_METADATA.description: Build metadata appended to the Mastodon version string (e.g. a commit SHA).

DB_HOST.description: PostgreSQL server hostname or Unix-socket directory.

DB_USER.description: PostgreSQL role name used to connect.

DB_POOL.description: Maximum number of database connections per process. Defaults to Sidekiq concurrency or MAX_THREADS.

PREPARED_STATEMENTS.description: Use prepared statements. Disable when using a connection pooler such as PgBouncer in transaction mode.

REPLICA_DB_HOST.description: Read-replica PostgreSQL hostname. Falls back to DB_HOST.

REPLICA_DB_NAME.description: Read-replica database name. Falls back to DB_NAME.

REPLICA_DB_USER.description: Read-replica role name. Falls back to DB_USER.

REPLICA_DB_PASS.description: Read-replica password. Falls back to DB_PASS.

REPLICA_PREPARED_STATEMENTS.description: Use prepared statements on the read replica. Falls back to PREPARED_STATEMENTS.

REPLICA_DB_TASKS.description: Run database schema tasks (e.g. db:schema:load) against the read replica as well as the primary.

REDIS_URL.description: Main Redis: full connection URL. When set, all other REDIS_* options are ignored.

REDIS_SENTINEL_MASTER.description: Main Redis: Sentinel master name. Required to enable Sentinel mode.

REDIS_SENTINELS.description: Main Redis: comma-separated list of Sentinel addresses (host:port).

REDIS_SENTINEL_PORT.description: Main Redis: default port for Sentinel nodes listed in REDIS_SENTINELS.

REDIS_SENTINEL_USERNAME.description: Main Redis: username for authenticating with Sentinels.

REDIS_SENTINEL_PASSWORD.description: Main Redis: password for authenticating with Sentinels.

SIDEKIQ_REDIS_URL.description: Sidekiq Redis: full connection URL. When set, all other SIDEKIQ_REDIS_* options are ignored.

SIDEKIQ_REDIS_SENTINEL_MASTER.description: Sidekiq Redis: Sentinel master name. Required to enable Sentinel mode.

SIDEKIQ_REDIS_SENTINELS.description: Sidekiq Redis: comma-separated list of Sentinel addresses (host:port).

SIDEKIQ_REDIS_SENTINEL_PORT.description: Sidekiq Redis: default port for Sentinel nodes listed in SIDEKIQ_REDIS_SENTINELS.

SIDEKIQ_REDIS_SENTINEL_USERNAME.description: Sidekiq Redis: username for authenticating with Sentinels.

SIDEKIQ_REDIS_SENTINEL_PASSWORD.description: Sidekiq Redis: password for authenticating with Sentinels.

CACHE_REDIS_URL.description: Cache Redis: full connection URL. When set, all other CACHE_REDIS_* options are ignored.

CACHE_REDIS_SENTINEL_MASTER.description: Cache Redis: Sentinel master name. Required to enable Sentinel mode.

CACHE_REDIS_SENTINELS.description: Cache Redis: comma-separated list of Sentinel addresses (host:port).

CACHE_REDIS_SENTINEL_PORT.description: Cache Redis: default port for Sentinel nodes listed in CACHE_REDIS_SENTINELS.

CACHE_REDIS_SENTINEL_USERNAME.description: Cache Redis: username for authenticating with Sentinels.

CACHE_REDIS_SENTINEL_PASSWORD.description: Cache Redis: password for authenticating with Sentinels.

REDIS_DRIVER.description: Redis client driver. "hiredis" is faster; "ruby" is a pure-Ruby fallback.

SMTP_DOMAIN.description: SMTP: HELO domain. Defaults to LOCAL_DOMAIN.

SMTP_CA_FILE.description: SMTP: path to the CA bundle used to verify the server certificate.

BULK_SMTP_DOMAIN.description: Bulk SMTP: HELO domain. Defaults to LOCAL_DOMAIN.

BULK_SMTP_TLS.description: Bulk SMTP: use implicit TLS (port 465 style).

BULK_SMTP_CA_FILE.description: Bulk SMTP: path to the CA bundle used to verify the server certificate.

BULK_SMTP_OPENSSL_VERIFY_MODE.description: Bulk SMTP: OpenSSL peer verification mode.

SMTP_ENABLE_STARTTLS_AUTO.description: SMTP: automatically negotiate STARTTLS if the server advertises it. Superseded by SMTP_ENABLE_STARTTLS when that is set.

BULK_SMTP_ENABLE_STARTTLS_AUTO.description: Bulk SMTP: automatically negotiate STARTTLS if the server advertises it. Superseded by BULK_SMTP_ENABLE_STARTTLS when that is set.

S3_ENABLED.description: Use S3-compatible object storage for media files.

S3_ENDPOINT.description: Override the S3 endpoint URL. Required for non-AWS providers such as MinIO, Wasabi, or Backblaze B2.

S3_HOSTNAME.description: Hostname used to construct public S3 URLs when no alias is set.

S3_ALIAS_HOST.description: Custom hostname (CDN / proxy) used for public media URLs, replacing the default S3 hostname.

S3_CLOUDFRONT_HOST.description: Alias for S3_ALIAS_HOST, kept for backwards compatibility.

S3_KEY_PREFIX.description: Prefix prepended to all S3 object keys.

S3_PERMISSION.description: Canned ACL applied to uploaded objects.

S3_PROTOCOL.description: Protocol used when constructing S3 URLs.

S3_OVERRIDE_PATH_STYLE.description: Force path-style S3 URLs (required for some non-AWS providers).

S3_OPEN_TIMEOUT.description: S3 connection open timeout in seconds.

S3_RETRY_LIMIT.description: Number of times to retry failed S3 requests.

S3_STORAGE_CLASS.description: S3 storage class for uploaded objects.

S3_MULTIPART_THRESHOLD.description: File size in bytes above which S3 uploads switch to multipart mode.

S3_FORCE_SINGLE_REQUEST.description: Disable multipart uploads and always use a single PUT request.

S3_ENABLE_CHECKSUM_MODE.description: Enable S3 checksum mode on object retrieval.

PAPERCLIP_ROOT_PATH.description: Filesystem path where media attachments are stored when using local storage.

PAPERCLIP_ROOT_URL.description: URL prefix for locally-stored media attachments.

EXTRA_MEDIA_HOSTS.description: Comma-separated list of additional hostnames allowed to serve media (added to the Content-Security-Policy).

SWIFT_OBJECT_URL.description: Public URL prefix for Swift objects.

SWIFT_CACHE_TTL.description: Swift metadata cache TTL in seconds.

AZURE_ALIAS_HOST.description: Custom hostname for public Azure media URLs.

ES_ENABLED.description: Enable Elasticsearch / OpenSearch for full-text search.

ES_PREFIX.description: Prefix applied to all Elasticsearch index names. Useful when sharing a cluster across multiple instances.

ES_CA_FILE.description: Path to a CA certificate bundle used to verify the Elasticsearch TLS connection.

ES_PRESET.description: Elasticsearch deployment preset. Use "single_node_cluster" when running a single-node cluster to avoid yellow health status.

OMNIAUTH_ONLY.description: Disallow password-based logins; require an external auth provider.

ONE_CLICK_SSO_LOGIN.description: Redirect unauthenticated users directly to the single configured OmniAuth provider instead of showing the sign-in form.

ALLOW_UNSAFE_AUTH_PROVIDER_REATTACH.description: Allow linking a new external auth identity to an existing account even when the e-mail does not match. Potentially dangerous.

SSO_ACCOUNT_SIGN_UP.description: URL of an external sign-up page shown to users whose SSO account does not yet exist in Mastodon.

SSO_ACCOUNT_SETTINGS.description: URL of an external account-settings page linked to in the Mastodon UI when SSO is active.

LDAP_METHOD.description: Transport security for the LDAP connection.

LDAP_BIND_DN.description: DN used to bind to the LDAP directory.

LDAP_PASSWORD.description: Password for the LDAP bind DN.

LDAP_UID.description: LDAP attribute used as the unique login identifier.

LDAP_MAIL.description: LDAP attribute containing the user's e-mail address.

LDAP_SEARCH_FILTER.description: LDAP search filter. The placeholders %<uid>s, %<mail>s, and %<email>s are substituted at runtime.

LDAP_TLS_NO_VERIFY.description: Skip TLS certificate verification when connecting to the LDAP server.

LDAP_UID_CONVERSION_ENABLED.description: Convert special characters in LDAP UIDs to underscores for Mastodon usernames.

LDAP_UID_CONVERSION_SEARCH.description: Characters in LDAP UIDs that should be replaced when UID conversion is enabled.

LDAP_UID_CONVERSION_REPLACE.description: Replacement character used when converting LDAP UIDs.

PAM_EMAIL_DOMAIN.description: Domain appended to PAM usernames to form e-mail addresses. Defaults to LOCAL_DOMAIN.

PAM_DEFAULT_SERVICE.description: PAM service name used for authentication.

PAM_CONTROLLED_SERVICE.description: Optional secondary PAM service that controls which users may register.

OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME.description: Label shown on the OIDC sign-in button.

OIDC_DISCOVERY.description: Fetch OIDC endpoints from the issuer's discovery document.

OIDC_SCOPE.description: Space-separated list of OIDC scopes to request.

OIDC_UID_FIELD.description: ID token / userinfo claim used as the stable unique identifier.

OIDC_REDIRECT_URI.description: OIDC redirect / callback URI registered with the provider.

OIDC_IDP_LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URI.description: URI to redirect to after the IdP logs the user out.

OIDC_SECURITY_ASSUME_EMAIL_IS_VERIFIED.description: Treat the OIDC e-mail claim as verified even when the email_verified claim is absent or false.

OIDC_CLIENT_AUTH_METHOD.description: Method used to authenticate the client with the token endpoint.

OIDC_USE_PKCE.description: Use PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) when requesting tokens.

OIDC_SEND_NONCE.description: Include a nonce in the OIDC authorization request.

OIDC_SEND_SCOPE_TO_TOKEN_ENDPOINT.description: Include the scope parameter when calling the OIDC token endpoint.

OIDC_DISPLAY.description: Hint to the OIDC provider about the display type for the authentication UI.

OIDC_PROMPT.description: Space-separated list of OIDC prompt values sent to the provider.

OIDC_HOST.description: OIDC provider hostname (used when discovery is disabled and individual endpoints are not set).

OIDC_HTTP_SCHEME.description: HTTP scheme used to construct OIDC endpoint URLs when OIDC_HOST is set.

SAML_DISPLAY_NAME.description: Label shown on the SAML sign-in button.

SAML_ACS_URL.description: Assertion Consumer Service URL (your instance's SAML callback endpoint).

SAML_IDP_CERT.description: PEM-encoded X.509 certificate of the IdP (used to verify assertion signatures).

SAML_CERT.description: PEM-encoded SP certificate (sent in metadata, used for encryption).

SAML_PRIVATE_KEY.description: PEM-encoded SP private key (used to sign requests / decrypt assertions).

SAML_SECURITY_WANT_ASSERTION_SIGNED.description: Require the IdP to sign SAML assertions.

SAML_SECURITY_WANT_ASSERTION_ENCRYPTED.description: Require the IdP to encrypt SAML assertions.

SAML_SECURITY_ASSUME_EMAIL_IS_VERIFIED.description: Treat the SAML e-mail attribute as verified.

SAML_IDP_CERT_FINGERPRINT.description: Fingerprint of the IdP certificate. Alternative to providing the full SAML_IDP_CERT.

SAML_IDP_CERT_FINGERPRINT_VALIDATOR.description: Ruby expression or proc used to validate the IdP certificate fingerprint dynamically.

SAML_IDP_SSO_TARGET_PARAMS.description: Extra query parameters appended to the IdP SSO URL at runtime.

SAML_NAME_IDENTIFIER_FORMAT.description: SAML NameID format requested from the IdP.

SAML_UID_ATTRIBUTE.description: SAML attribute used as the stable unique identifier for the user.

SAML_ALLOWED_CLOCK_DRIFT.description: Permitted clock skew in seconds when validating SAML assertion timestamps.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_UID.description: SAML attribute name that maps to the user UID.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_EMAIL.description: SAML attribute name that maps to the user e-mail address.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_FULL_NAME.description: SAML attribute name that maps to the user full name.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_FIRST_NAME.description: SAML attribute name that maps to the user first name.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_LAST_NAME.description: SAML attribute name that maps to the user last name.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_VERIFIED.description: SAML attribute name that indicates whether the user is verified.

SAML_ATTRIBUTES_STATEMENTS_VERIFIED_EMAI.description: SAML attribute name that contains a verified e-mail address.

CAS_DISPLAY_NAME.description: Label shown on the CAS sign-in button.

CAS_HOST.description: CAS server hostname (used when CAS_URL is not set).

CAS_SECURITY_ASSUME_EMAIL_IS_VERIFIED.description: Treat the CAS e-mail attribute as verified.

CAS_CALLBACK_URL.description: CAS callback URL (your instance receives the ticket here).

CAS_CA_PATH.description: Path to a CA certificate bundle used to verify the CAS server TLS connection.

CAS_DISABLE_SSL_VERIFICATION.description: Disable TLS certificate verification when connecting to the CAS server.

CAS_UID_FIELD.description: CAS attribute used as the unique identifier for the user.

CAS_NAME_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user display name.

CAS_EMAIL_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user e-mail address.

CAS_NICKNAME_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user nickname / username.

CAS_FIRST_NAME_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user first name.

CAS_LAST_NAME_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user last name.

CAS_LOCATION_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user location.

CAS_IMAGE_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user avatar image URL.

CAS_PHONE_KEY.description: CAS attribute that maps to the user phone number.

PORT.description: TCP port the Puma web server listens on.

BIND.description: IP address Puma binds to when using TCP.

SOCKET.description: Unix socket path for Puma. When set, TCP PORT / BIND are ignored.

MAX_THREADS.description: Maximum number of threads per Puma worker.

MIN_THREADS.description: Minimum number of threads per Puma worker. Defaults to MAX_THREADS.

PERSISTENT_TIMEOUT.description: Seconds to keep idle HTTP keep-alive connections open.

TRUSTED_PROXY_IP.description: Comma or space-separated list of trusted reverse-proxy IP addresses / CIDR ranges.

SENDFILE_HEADER.description: Header used by the web server to serve files directly (e.g. "X-Accel-Redirect" for nginx).

CDN_HOST.description: CDN hostname used to construct asset URLs.

LOCAL_HTTPS.description: Force HTTPS scheme in generated URLs even in non-production environments.

DISALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED_API_ACCESS.description: Require an OAuth token for all API requests, even those that would normally be public.

MAX_REQUEST_POOL_SIZE.description: Maximum size of the outgoing HTTP request connection pool.

QUERY_LOG_TAGS_ENABLED.description: Annotate SQL queries with the controller/action that triggered them (useful for debugging; slight performance cost).

MASTODON_SIDEKIQ_READY_FILENAME.description: Path to a file created by Sidekiq when it is ready to process jobs. Used for Kubernetes readiness probes.

REDIS_NAMESPACE.description: Prefix all Redis keys with this namespace. Deprecated — use separate Redis databases instead.

ALLOW_ACCESS_TO_HIDDEN_SERVICE.description: Allow Mastodon to connect to .onion addresses via the HTTP proxy.

SECRET_KEY_BASE.description: Rails secret key base. Used to sign and encrypt session cookies. Must be a long random hex string. Generate with `bundle exec rails secret`.

OTP_SECRET.description: Legacy TOTP secret. Required only when migrating from older Mastodon versions.

ACTIVE_RECORD_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_K.description: Active Record Encryption deterministic key. Generate with `bin/rails db:encryption:init`. Must remain constant for the lifetime of the database.

ACTIVE_RECORD_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_.description: Active Record Encryption key-derivation salt. Must remain constant for the lifetime of the database.

ACTIVE_RECORD_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY.description: Active Record Encryption primary key. Must remain constant for the lifetime of the database.

VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY.description: VAPID private key for Web Push notifications. Generate with `bundle exec rails mastodon:webpush:generate_vapid_key`.

VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY.description: VAPID public key for Web Push notifications. Must correspond to VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY.

AUTHORIZED_FETCH.description: Require HTTP Signature authentication for all ActivityPub fetch requests, preventing anonymous scraping of public content.

DISABLE_FOLLOWERS_SYNCHRONIZATION.description: Disable the periodic followers-collection synchronisation (reduces federation traffic at the cost of potential divergence).

MAX_FOLLOWS_THRESHOLD.description: Account follow count above which the follow/follower ratio limit is enforced.

MAX_FOLLOWS_RATIO.description: Maximum ratio of following-to-followers once an account exceeds MAX_FOLLOWS_THRESHOLD.

MAX_SESSION_ACTIVATIONS.description: Maximum number of simultaneous active sessions per user account.

DISABLE_AUTOMATIC_SWITCHING_TO_APPROVED_.description: Prevent Mastodon from automatically switching to approval-required registration when a spam wave is detected.

DEFAULT_LOCALE.description: BCP 47 language tag used as the default interface locale.

EMAIL_DOMAIN_DENYLIST.description: Pipe-separated list of e-mail domains to reject during registration.

EMAIL_DOMAIN_ALLOWLIST.description: Pipe-separated list of e-mail domains to accept during registration. When set, all other domains are rejected.

EMAIL_DOMAIN_LISTS_APPLY_AFTER_CONFIRMAT.description: Apply e-mail domain lists at the confirmation step rather than at registration.

VIPS_BLOCK_UNTRUSTED.description: Block untrusted/complex libvips image operations (security hardening).

ALLOWED_PRIVATE_ADDRESSES.description: Comma-separated list of private IP addresses / CIDR ranges that Mastodon is allowed to connect to (e.g. for on-premises services).

PROXY_PROTO_V1.description: Enable PROXY protocol v1 support for the streaming server.

IP_RETENTION_PERIOD.description: How long (in seconds) to retain IP addresses associated with user sign-ups and logins.

SESSION_RETENTION_PERIOD.description: How long (in seconds) to retain browser session data.

USER_ACTIVE_DAYS.description: Number of days of inactivity after which a user is no longer counted as "active".

DEEPL_API_KEY.description: DeepL API key for the built-in translation feature.

DEEPL_PLAN.description: DeepL subscription plan. Determines which API endpoint is used.

HCAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY.description: hCaptcha secret key. Enables captcha challenges during registration when set.

HCAPTCHA_SITE_KEY.description: hCaptcha site key (public, sent to the browser).

CACHE_BUSTER_ENABLED.description: Enable CDN cache purge requests when media is updated or deleted.

CACHE_BUSTER_SECRET_HEADER.description: HTTP header name used to authenticate cache purge requests.

CACHE_BUSTER_HTTP_METHOD.description: HTTP method used for cache purge requests.

MASTODON_PROMETHEUS_EXPORTER_HOST.description: Hostname or IP the Prometheus exporter server binds to.

MASTODON_PROMETHEUS_EXPORTER_PORT.description: Port the Prometheus exporter server listens on.

MASTODON_PROMETHEUS_EXPORTER_LOCAL.description: Run a local Prometheus exporter server inside each process instead of using a shared collector.

OTEL_SERVICE_NAME_PREFIX.description: Prefix for the OpenTelemetry service name.

OTEL_SERVICE_NAME_SEPARATOR.description: Character used to join the service name prefix and the process-type suffix.

FFMPEG_BINARY.description: Path to the ffmpeg binary used for video transcoding.

FFPROBE_BINARY.description: Path to the ffprobe binary used to inspect video files.

S3_BATCH_DELETE_LIMIT.description: Maximum number of objects deleted in a single S3 batch-delete request.

S3_BATCH_DELETE_RETRY.description: Number of retries for failed S3 batch-delete operations.
